summary: Twining conditions use SDK plugin system with zero extra instructions — plugin alone drives agent behavior
context: Initial implementation added custom system prompts and CLAUDE.md Twining sections that duplicated plugin functionality. Agents had double instructions but still weren't using tools reliably because the raw MCP server lacked the plugin infrastructure (hooks, skills, BEHAVIORS.md).
rationale: Loading Twining as a local plugin via SDK plugins option gives agents the full infrastructure: SessionStart hook forcing twining_assemble, Stop hook enforcing decisions/status, skills teaching workflow, BEHAVIORS.md with tool usage spec. Removing all harness-side Twining instructions ensures the benchmark tests what a real user gets by simply installing the plugin — no extra prompting or hand-holding.

alternatives.option: Keep raw MCP server with enhanced system prompts
alternatives.reason_rejected: Agents ignored system prompt instructions without plugin hooks to enforce them. Also tests an artificial setup no real user would have.

alternatives.option: Use plugin but also add harness-side system prompt reinforcement
alternatives.reason_rejected: Duplicates plugin instructions and gives Twining condition an unfair advantage via extra guidance other conditions don't get.
